prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of ferrocene (21 atoms: Fe, 10 C, 10 H) computed using the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding method (Bannwarth et al. 2019) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Partial charges were obtained via the Mulliken scheme at the GFN2-xTB level. The N±1 states used charge ±1 and multiplicity 2. The geometry was taken directly from the supplied ferrocene.xyz (no re-optimization). The headline value reported is the maximum f⁻ (electrophilic attack Fukui function), located on the Fe center (atom 11): f⁻(Fe) = 0.25938191. Fe is also the most nucleophilic site (f⁺ = 0.22885456) and has a negative dual descriptor (Δf = −0.030527), consistent with its role as the dominant nucleophilic center in the molecule. All Cp-ring C and H atoms are symmetry-equivalent within each ring and carry small, nearly identical Fukui values. Integrity checks all passed: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000. No warnings were reported. Trustworthy = true.
